Indian bonds choppy as oil reverses course putting focus back on US-Iran issues
items.content: Indian government bond yields edged higher in early trading Tuesday, influenced by renewed U.S.-Iran tensions that pushed oil prices up. This development reversed the support from softer Treasury yields, creating a choppy market environment. Traders anticipate a narrow trading range for bonds, with Friday's auction potentially swaying sentiment.

items.title: Policy stability, relief on STT and LTCG key to winning back FPIs: Sudip Bandyopadhyay
items.content: Foreign investors are pulling money from Indian markets. Experts urge India to rethink taxes like STT and LTCG. Policy consistency is also a major concern for global funds. While domestic investors provide support, foreign capital remains vital for growth. Adjusting these tax structures could boost investor confidence and market stability.

USC scientists discover a hidden Alzheimer’s trigger and a possible way to shut it down
items.content: USC researchers have identified potential new drug compounds that may reduce the brain inflammation linked to Alzheimer’s disease, especially in people with the high-risk APOE4 gene. The compounds target cPLA2, an enzyme that seems to fuel harmful inflammation while also being important for normal brain activity.

items.title: This prehistoric fish may explain how animals first walked on Earth
items.content: Scientists have peered inside the skull of a 380-million-year-old Antarctic fish that was closely related to the first animals to walk on land, revealing surprising clues about how life began its move out of the water. Using advanced neutron imaging, researchers discovered that Koharalepis jarviki had features suited for living near the water’s surface, including openings in its skull that may have helped it gulp air and a light-sensitive organ linked to day-night rhythms.
items.summary: Scientists have peered inside the skull of a 380-million-year-old Antarctic fish that was closely related to the first animals to walk on land, revealing surprising clues about how life began its move...

items.title: The pope’s AI encyclical isn’t really about AI
items.content: Pope Leo XIV's first encyclical uses AI as a lens to diagnose older problems: concentrated power, eroding democracy, and a tech elite that shapes the world to its own advantage.
